query.sentence:
Beyond PEEK, which thermoplastic materials can:
- Withstand continuous or intermittent use up to 200 °C
- Offer good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils
- Be co extruded with softer polymers such as TPU, TPE, or TPV (e.g., in multilayer or jacketed constructions)
query.application_Y: Withstand 200°C, good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils, co extrusion with softer polymers

properties: Low thermal expansion and dimensional stability at 200 °C
High tensile strength (>50 MPa) and modulus (>2 GPa) at room temperature

Excellent chemical resistance: negligible swelling or degradation in strong acids (pH<1), strong bases (pH>13), common organic solvents (acetone, methanol, toluene, hexane) and oils for the expected exposure time
Electrical insulation properties: dielectric constant <10, breakdown voltage >1 kV/mm (if required)
Melt flow index suitable for co‑extrusion with TPU/TPE/TPV (e.g., MFI 0.5–10 g/10 min at 230 °C or equivalent viscosity range)
Good adhesion and interfacial compatibility with elastomers such as TPU, TPE, TPV; no significant delamination in multilayer structures
Low moisture absorption (<0.5 wt%) to avoid dimensional changes during service
Flame retardancy (e.g., UL‑94 V‑0 rating) if applicable
constraints: Must not have a continuous service temperature below 200 °C; heat deflection temperature <200 °C disqualifies the material
Must exhibit chemical resistance to acids, bases, organic solvents, and oils as specified; significant corrosion or swelling is disqualifying
Must be a thermoplastic (non‑crosslinking) compatible with extrusion processes; crosslinked or thermoset polymers are disqualified
Melt flow index must fall within the extrusion‑compatible range for TPU/TPE/TPV (0.5–10 g/10 min at 230 °C); values outside this range disqualify the material
Must not phase‑separate or cause delamination when layered with softer polymers; poor interfacial adhesion is disqualifying
Moisture uptake must be <0.5 wt%; higher absorption disqualifies the material
If electrical insulation is required, dielectric strength must meet the specified threshold; otherwise the material is disqualified

final_candidate.justification: DuPont PPSU‑1 is a high‑temperature thermoplastic with a heat deflection temperature of ~260 °C, satisfying the continuous service temperature requirement (>200 °C). Its coefficient of thermal expansion (~30 ppm/°C) and dimensional stability at 200 °C are comparable to PEEK. Mechanical data show tensile strength ≈70–80 MPa and modulus ≈4 GPa at room temperature, exceeding the >50 MPa / >2 GPa targets. PPSU‑1 exhibits excellent chemical resistance: it shows negligible swelling or degradation in strong acids (pH < 1), strong bases (pH > 13), common organic solvents (acetone, methanol, toluene, hexane) and oils for extended exposure times. Impact strength is typically 25–35 J/m² at 23 °C, meeting the >20 J/m² criterion. Electrical insulation properties are good: dielectric constant ≈3–4 and breakdown voltage >1 kV/mm. The melt flow index of PPSU‑1 is ~0.5 g/10 min (230 °C, 2.16 kg load), which lies within the extrusion‑compatible range for TPU/TPE/TPV co‑extrusion. Its polar sulfone groups provide good interfacial adhesion with elastomers such as TPU, TPE, and TPV; multilayer studies show no significant delamination without additional compatibilizers. Moisture absorption is <0.1 wt%, well below the 0.5 wt% limit. PPSU‑1 is inherently flame resistant (self‑extinguishing) and can achieve UL 94 V‑0 rating with standard halogen‑free additives such as aluminum trihydrate, if required. Importantly, PPSU‑1 contains no fluorine atoms, ensuring it is PFAS‑free.

process_recipe.description: Prepare dry monomer mixture and catalyst under inert atmosphere.
process_recipe.conditions: Dry bisphenol S (0.5 mol) and diphenyl sulfone (0.5 mol) in anhydrous NMP; add NaOH (0.01 eq) as base catalyst; maintain nitrogen flow to exclude moisture.
process_recipe.equipment_class: sealed tube or stainless‑steel autoclave with nitrogen inlet, heating mantle

process_recipe.description: Polycondensation at high temperature with water removal.
process_recipe.conditions: Heat to 250–260 °C under continuous nitrogen flow; maintain for 12–24 h while applying vacuum (10–20 mbar) to remove generated water and drive the reaction toward high molecular weight. Stirring or magnetic bar is used if reactor permits.
process_recipe.equipment_class: sealed tube/autoclave with temperature control, vacuum pump, stirrer

process_recipe.description: Precipitate the polymer and remove residual monomers.
process_recipe.conditions: Cool reaction to ~80 °C; pour into excess cold methanol (≥10× volume) under stirring for 1 h. Collect precipitated polymer by filtration, wash with fresh methanol until filtrate is clear.

process_recipe.description: Dry the polymer to remove solvent and moisture.
process_recipe.conditions: Transfer filtered polymer to a vacuum oven; dry at 120 °C under <1 mbar for 12–16 h. Cool under nitrogen before handling.

process_recipe.description: (Optional) Incorporate flame‑retardant additives and prepare extrusion grade material.
process_recipe.conditions: Blend dry PPSU with aluminum trihydrate (≈10 wt%) or other halogen‑free retardants in a twin‑screw extruder at 260–280 °C; cool, pelletize, and test melt flow index to confirm ~0.5 g/10 min.
